question: According to Manaud 2024, what is the relative density (percentage) of TaC sintered by spark plasma sintering at 1873 K?
reference_answer: The relative density of TaC sintered at 1873 K is 96 ± 1%.
evidence_any: Dense tantalum-based carbonitride materials featuring greater than 95 percent relative density TaC
The values reported in Table 1 show that the relative densities of the Ta2CN pellets
excerpts:
Table 1 Relative densities of pellets after sintering by SPS from TaC to TaN with the three intermediate compounds.

Composition Tsintering (K) Relative density (%)

TaC 1873 96 ± 1 Ta2C1.5N0.5 1873 95 ± 2 Ta2CN 1873 97 ± 1 Ta2CN 1973 97 ± 1 Ta2C0.5N1.5 1873 96 ± 1 TaN 1873 95 ± 2

The values reported in Table 1 show that the relative densities of the Ta2CN pellets are the same meaning that a sintering temperature as low as 1873 K appears sufficient for our study.

question: In the Manaud 2024 study, what is the relative density (percentage) of the Ta2CN carbonitride when sintered at 1973 K?
reference_answer: The relative density of Ta2CN sintered at 1973 K is 97 ± 1%.
evidence_any: then we performed the sintering of a series of samples from TaC to TaN
To decide which temperature to select for the rest of the study we checked
excerpts:
Table 1 Relative densities of pellets after sintering by SPS from TaC to TaN with the three intermediate compounds.

Composition Tsintering (K) Relative density (%)

TaC 1873 96 ± 1 Ta2C1.5N0.5 1873 95 ± 2 Ta2CN 1873 97 ± 1 Ta2CN 1973 97 ± 1 Ta2C0.5N1.5 1873 96 ± 1 TaN 1873 95 ± 2

To decide which temperature to select for the rest of the study we checked the relative density of the Ta2CN pellets prepared at 1873 K and 1973 K. The values reported in Table 1 show that the relative densities of the Ta2CN pellets are the same.

question: What is the Young's modulus in GPa of TaC as measured by nano-indentation in the Manaud 2024 study?
reference_answer: The Young's modulus of TaC measured by nano-indentation is 704 ± 35 GPa, which is higher than typical literature values for similar UHTC materials.
evidence_any: The Young's moduli measured by nano indentation were approx 600 700 GPa
The TaC sample exhibited a higher value of Young modulus 704 plus minus 35 GPa
excerpts:
Table 3 Young's modulus and hardness of the different samples averaged among all the tests results.

Sample Young's Modulus (GPa) Hardness (GPa)

TaC 704 ± 35 16.4 ± 0.8 Ta2C1.5N0.5 606 ± 37 20.5 ± 1.5 Ta2CN 609 ± 29 22.9 ± 1.2 Ta2C0.5N1.5 648 ± 32 26.5 ± 2.2 TaN 634 ± 23 27.9 ± 1.3

The TaC sample exhibited a higher value of Young modulus of 704 ± 35 GPa than the rest of the series ranging from 606 to 648 GPa. Indeed the tantalum carbonitride samples have a Young modulus closer to TaN.

question: According to Manaud 2024, what is the hardness in GPa of the TaN tantalum nitride sample measured by nano-indentation?
reference_answer: The hardness of TaN measured by nano-indentation is 27.9 ± 1.3 GPa, representing the highest hardness value in the tantalum carbonitride series.
evidence_any: The hardness values increased from 16.4 0.8 GPa for TaC to 27.9 1.3 GPa for TaN
TaN possess the highest hardness with an approximately linear trend
excerpts:
Table 3 Young's modulus and hardness of the different samples averaged among all the tests results.

Sample Young's Modulus (GPa) Hardness (GPa)

TaC 704 ± 35 16.4 ± 0.8 Ta2C1.5N0.5 606 ± 37 20.5 ± 1.5 Ta2CN 609 ± 29 22.9 ± 1.2 Ta2C0.5N1.5 648 ± 32 26.5 ± 2.2 TaN 634 ± 23 27.9 ± 1.3

TaC exhibit the lowest hardness 16.4 ± 0.8 GPa, while TaN possesses the highest hardness 27.9 ± 1.3 GPa within this set. In between the carbonitride materials show an approximately linear trend with hardness increasing with increasing nitride content.

question: What is the hardness value in GPa of the Ta2CN carbonitride composition in the Manaud 2024 nano-indentation study?
reference_answer: The hardness of Ta2CN measured by nano-indentation is 22.9 ± 1.2 GPa.
evidence_any: the carbonitride materials show an approximately linear trend with hardness increasing
Table 3 summarizes the Young modulus and hardness results as averages
excerpts:
Table 3 Young's modulus and hardness of the different samples averaged among all the tests results.

Sample Young's Modulus (GPa) Hardness (GPa)

TaC 704 ± 35 16.4 ± 0.8 Ta2C1.5N0.5 606 ± 37 20.5 ± 1.5 Ta2CN 609 ± 29 22.9 ± 1.2 Ta2C0.5N1.5 648 ± 32 26.5 ± 2.2 TaN 634 ± 23 27.9 ± 1.3

In between the carbonitride materials show an approximately linear trend with hardness increasing with increasing nitride content. The values of hardness measured on these samples are in line with reported hardness values for UHTC materials.

question: In the Manaud 2024 study, what is the unit cell parameter a in Angstroms for cubic TaC determined by Rietveld refinement from PXRD data?
reference_answer: The unit cell parameter a for TaC determined by Rietveld refinement is 4.4430(1) Å.
evidence_any: TaC has a unit cell parameter a equal 4.4430 1 Angstrom
The PXRD diagrams of the sintered TaC confirm the cubic structure
excerpts:
Table 2 Crystallography parameters determined by Rietveld refinement on PXRD diagrams and microstructural parameters measured on ion-polished cross-sections of each sample from TaC to TaN after SPS sintering.

Sample Phase Structure Unit cell parameters (Å) Volume fraction

TaC TaC Cubic 4.4430(1) 0.92 0.98 1.79 4.1 5.2

The PXRD diagrams of the sintered TaC Ta2C1.5N0.5 and Ta2CN pellets show only one phase. The TaC sample is made of the cubic Fm-3m TaC.

question: What is the median grain diameter d50 in micrometers of the main TaC phase in the Manaud 2024 EBSD/EDS analysis?
reference_answer: The median grain diameter d50 of the main TaC phase is 1.79 micrometers.
evidence_any: The d50 of the main phase TaC or Ta2CxNy decreases monotonically
The size distributions of the main phases listed in Table 2
excerpts:
Table 2 Crystallography parameters determined by Rietveld refinement on PXRD diagrams and microstructural parameters measured on ion-polished cross-sections of each sample from TaC to TaN after SPS sintering.

Sample Phase Structure Unit cell parameters (Å) Volume fraction d50 (μm)

TaC TaC Cubic 4.4430(1) 0.92 0.98 1.79 4.1 5.2

The size distributions calculated based upon equivalent diameter filling with overlapping disks showed that the d50 of the main phase TaC or Ta2CxNy decreases monotonically by a factor of approximately two between the TaC and Ta2CN sample.

question: According to Jayaseelan 2011, what is the initial total porosity percentage of the porous C/C composite tubes measured using the Archimedes method?
reference_answer: The porous C/C composite tubes had an initial total porosity of approximately 60%, measured using the Archimedes method.
evidence_any: The porous C/C composite tubes had a total porosity of about 60 measured
measured using Archimedes method and bimodal pore size distribution
excerpts: Porous C/C tubes of 17.75 mm OD times 3.90 mm ID times 45 mm long were used in the present study for ZrB2 infiltration. The porous C/C composite tubes had a total porosity of approximately 60 percent (measured using Archimedes method) and bimodal pore size distribution (determined using laser diffraction particle size analyser) with average pore sizes of 150 micrometers and 10 micrometers, respectively.

question: What is the solid loading percentage of ZrB2 in the slurry used for infiltration in the Jayaseelan 2011 reactive infiltration study?
reference_answer: The slurry used for reactive infiltration of C/C composite tubes contained 40 to 50 percent ZrB2 solid loading particles, with the gel composite approach using 50 weight percent total solid loading.
evidence_any: A slurry with 40 50 percent ZrB2 solid loading particles was used
Wet slurry containing 50 weight percent solid loading of ZrB2 was prepared
excerpts: A novel reactive infiltration processing technique was employed to infiltrate porous carbon fibre reinforced carbon C C composite hollow tubes with ultra high temperature ceramic UHTC particles such as ZrB2. The C C composite tubes had initial porosity of approximately 60 percent with a bimodal 10 micrometer and 100 micrometer pore size distribution. A slurry with 40 to 50 percent ZrB2 solid loading particles was used to infiltrate the C C tubes.
